frameworks.name: Bayesian inference
frameworks.one_line: normative framework for decision-making under uncertainty in the brain

frameworks.name: modular neural implementation
frameworks.one_line: probabilistic components independently encoded across neural substrates

frameworks.name: transform neural implementation
frameworks.one_line: uncertain measurements converted to Bayesian estimates via latent processes

core_question: How does the brain neurally implement Bayesian inference?
key_assumptions: organisms make decisions under uncertainty following normative Bayesian theories
neural implementations can be taxonomized by how probabilistic components are represented
key_findings: recent experiments reveal disparate neural mechanisms producing Bayesian-predicted behavior
implementations cluster into modular vs transform classes
many recent probabilistic inference findings fall into these two classes
interpretations: distinguishing among implementation hypotheses requires joint theory-experiment work across scales, circuits, tasks, and species
some disparities between the two classes cannot presently be reconciled
limitations_noted: current disparities across implementations cannot yet be reconciled
